# consumer-tasks-graph fixture (T030)

This is the FR-013 non-regression tripwire for `/speckit-tasks-graph`'s specific consumption of `graphify-context.md` — consumer fixture 2 of 3 (siblings: `consumer-plan/` for T029, `consumer-implement/` for T031), each protecting one named consumer of the shared diet per golden-fixture-discipline, so a regression in any single consumer's derivation logic is caught even while the other two stay green. `speckit-tasks-graph/SKILL.md` derives its `[P]` parallel markers and `## Execution Waves` DAG from exactly two `graphify-context.md` sections — `## Blast radius (per anchor)` (the per-file depends-on / depended-on-by edges Step D checks at the symbol level) and `## Shared / mutable files (collision watch)` (the collision list Step D and the Wave rules both cite to force serialization) — and `cmd.sh` asserts, in 14 individually named PASS/FAIL checks across four groups, that this chain is unbroken end to end: the generator's own Output template in `speckit-graphify-context/SKILL.md` still emits both section headings with their documented bullet shapes (`depends on:` / `depended on by:` / the "must be serialized" guidance); the worked `examples/graphify-context.example.md` still mirrors both headings byte-for-byte and populates each with real, non-placeholder content; `speckit-tasks-graph/SKILL.md` itself still names `graphify-context.md` as what it reads (Step A), still ties `[P]` stripping to the shared/mutable list "in `graphify-context.md`" (Step D), and still states the wave rule that pulls any shared/mutable-file task into a serial wave; and the worked `examples/tasks-with-waves.example.md` still demonstrates the derivation actually landing — two tasks (T011, T021) that both touch `src/api/routes.ts` losing their naive `[P]` and being pinned into the same serial wave. Because every check greps a section-scoped excerpt (via `sed` range extraction) rather than the whole file, a check can only pass on real content under its own heading, never on an unrelated bullet borrowed from elsewhere in the document — verified by mutating throwaway copies (never the repo tree) to confirm each check flips to FAIL for the regression it targets, then confirming the real files pass again unmutated. This fixture needs no script-under-test, no graph.json, and no LLM (the "consumer" being protected is a natural-language `SKILL.md` read by an agent at runtime, not a binary), so it is GREEN now and stays a live guard from here on; it breaks the moment any one of these four documents changes the diet's parallelism-relevant shape.
